Phase 2c reference vector — t2_cache_derive_item_batch.bin
===========================================================

Spec attestation
----------------
T2 attests Rust `Cache::derive_item` byte-for-byte parity with the
v2 RandomX fork's `randomx::initDatasetItem` for 8 ad-hoc sample
indices chosen to exercise the superscalar chain over a spread of
input magnitudes. `initDatasetItem` / `Cache::derive_item` accept
arbitrary `u64` item numbers (they consume the input as initial
register state for the superscalar program; no input-side mask is
applied at this entry point — only the inner `Cache::item_bytes`
cache reads mask, and they mask to the cache's 22-bit row index
range), so these 8 indices are sample points, not spec boundaries
in the sense of constants the algorithm switches on.

The 8 indices:

  - item 0 (lowest input — initial register state at zero entropy)
  - item 1 (lowest + 1, exercises the low-bit transition)
  - item 1023 / 1024 (10-bit transition, 2^10 - 1 / 2^10)
  - item 524287 / 524288 — **this pair IS a spec-anchored
    boundary**: 524287 = `DATASET_EXTRA_ITEMS` (`= RANDOMX_DATASET_
    EXTRA_SIZE / CACHE_LINE_SIZE = 33,554,368 / 64`), the upper
    edge of the per-VM dataset-offset window that
    `VmState::init_program` derives from entropy[13] (see
    `vm.rs:1173`). 524288 = `DATASET_EXTRA_ITEMS + 1` (just past it).
  - item 2097150 / 2097151 (21-bit transition, 2^21 - 2 / 2^21 - 1
    — additional high-magnitude sample points; NOT a spec boundary,
    NOT the "highest valid items". The full RandomX dataset spans
    ~34M items (`(RANDOMX_DATASET_BASE_SIZE + RANDOMX_DATASET_
    EXTRA_SIZE) / CACHE_LINE_SIZE ≈ 34,078,720`); and the cache's
    own row count is `DATASET_ITEM_COUNT = 4,194,304` (22-bit mask
    space used by `Cache::item_bytes`). 2097151 is well below both.)

A divergence at any of the 8 attributes the failure to the specific
input magnitude that failed; the 524287 / 524288 pair specifically
gates the `DATASET_EXTRA_ITEMS` boundary used by `init_program`.

Per RANDOMX_V2_PHASE2C_PLAN.md §5.7 (F7 T2).

Inputs (hardcoded in `_generator/phase2c/gen.cpp`)
--------------------------------------------------
seedhash       = [0x01, 0x02, ..., 0x20]  (32-byte canonical)
item_numbers   = [0, 1, 1023, 1024, 524287, 524288, 2097150, 2097151]
flag           = RANDOMX_FLAG_DEFAULT

Output layout
-------------
8 × 64-byte dataset items, concatenated in `item_numbers` order:

  +0   item 0           (64 B)
  +64  item 1           (64 B)
  +128 item 1023        (64 B)
  +192 item 1024        (64 B)
  +256 item 524287      (64 B)
  +320 item 524288      (64 B)
  +384 item 2097150     (64 B)
  +448 item 2097151     (64 B)

Each 64-byte item is 8 × u64 little-endian — the natural output
shape of `initDatasetItem` (which writes via `store64` to the
caller's cache-line buffer).

Total bytes for this vector: 512 (= 8 × 64).

Generator
---------
source      = ./_generator/phase2c/gen.cpp (mode t2)
build       = ./_generator/phase2c/Makefile (`make gen`)
command     = ./gen t2 > t2_cache_derive_item_batch.bin
output_size = 512 bytes

Reference impl
--------------
fork        = external/randomx-v2/
pin         = aaafe71322df6602c21a5c72937ac284724ae561 (v2.0.1)
sources     = src/dataset.cpp (initDatasetItem),
              src/superscalar.hpp (executeSuperscalar),
              src/reciprocal.c
function    = randomx::initDatasetItem(randomx_cache*, uint8_t*, uint64_t)

Phase
-----
Phase 2c (`docs/design/RANDOMX_V2_PHASE2C_PLAN.md` §5.7 / F7 T2,
§9 commit 7) — dataset item derivation parity. Consumed by Rust
test `t2_cache_derive_item_batch_matches_fork_reference` in
`rust/shekyl-pow-randomx/src/cache.rs#mod tests`.
